background: The Youth Risk Behavior Surveillance System monitors six categories of health behaviors in U.S. youth and adolescents. Many types of behaviors that contribute to adverse health consequences are monitored.

task.query: As examples, the proportion of high school students who had ridden with a driver who had been drinking alcohol during the preceding 30-day period was $30.2 \%$, the proportion who had smoked cigarettes was $21.9 \%$, and the proportion who were overweight was $13.5 \%$. These values are statistical estimates of population parameters.
